e-system.content:
### Legibility as a System

Legibility is not a property of typefaces — it is a property of typographic systems. A typeface that performs well in body text at 16px on a white background may become illegible at 11px on a saturated colour, or at 18px on a dark surface with insufficient line-height.

This means that type selection and type setting are inseparable decisions. Choosing a typeface without knowing the conditions it will be used in is like choosing a material without knowing what loads it will carry. The choice must be made in context.

e-quick-ref.content:
### Quick Reference

**Body text minimum** — 15–16px screen, 9pt print

**Line length** — 55–75 characters for comfortable reading

**Line height** — 1.4–1.6× body size; increase for smaller text and dark backgrounds

**Contrast minimum** — 4.5:1 (AA) for body text, 3:1 for large text

**Letter-spacing** — Tighten display; never tighten body; add tracking to all-caps

**Weights** — Regular for body; medium or semibold for UI labels; bold for display only

**Variable fonts** — Prefer where available; enables responsive adjustments without multiple font files

e-reading.content:
### Further Reading

Bringhurst, R. — *The Elements of Typographic Style* (1992)

Lupton, E. — *Thinking with Type* (2004)

Tracy, W. — *Letters of Credit* (1986)

Michaelides, M. — *The Legibility of Type* (2003)

W3C — *Understanding Success Criterion 1.4.8* (WCAG 2.1)

Google Fonts — *Type and Legibility: Knowledge Articles* (2022)

Sindermann, C. et al. — *Screen Reading and Typographic Quality* (2022)
